tools.name: changelog
tools.description: Generate a changelog from git commit history between two refs. Use when the user asks for release notes, a changelog, or a summary of changes between tags/branches.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

from.description: Starting ref (tag, branch, commit). Default: previous tag.

to.description: Ending ref (tag, branch, commit). Default: HEAD.

tools.name: git_branch
tools.description: Git branch management: list, create, switch, or delete branches. Use when the user asks to list branches, create a new branch, checkout/switch, or delete a branch.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

action.description: What to do: list branches, create a new one, switch to one, or delete one.

name.description: Branch name (required for create, switch, delete).

base.description: Base branch to create from (for 'create' action). Defaults to current branch.

force.description: For 'delete': force-delete an unmerged branch (git branch -D). Without this, deleting an unmerged branch fails safely. Default false.

tools.name: git_commit
tools.description: Stage files and create a git commit. Use when the user asks to commit changes, stage files, or save work. WARNING: This modifies the repository. The assistant should confirm the commit message with the user before calling.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

files.description: Specific files to stage. If omitted, stages all changes (git add -A).

amend.description: Amend the previous commit instead of creating a new one.

tools.name: git_diff
tools.description: Show git diff: changes between commits, working tree, and staged area. Use when the user asks to see a diff, review changes, or compare branches.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

staged.description: Show only staged (cached) changes. Equivalent to --cached.

ref_a.description: First ref (branch, commit, tag) to compare. If omitted, shows working tree changes.

ref_b.description: Second ref to compare against ref_a. Omit for working-tree diff.

paths.description: Limit diff to specific file paths.

stat.description: Show only diffstat (insertions/deletions summary) instead of full diff.

tools.name: git_log
tools.description: Show git commit log with configurable format and count. Use when the user asks for recent commits, git log, or commit history.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

count.description: Maximum number of commits to show. Default 20.

branch.description: Branch or ref to show log for. Defaults to HEAD.

since.description: Show commits more recent than this date (e.g. '2 weeks ago', '2024-01-01').

tools.name: git_push
tools.description: Push the current (or a named) branch to a remote, setting upstream tracking on first push. Use when the user asks to push commits, publish a branch, or before opening a PR on a branch that has not been pushed yet. WARNING: This updates a remote. The assistant should confirm before pushing, especially when force-pushing.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

branch.description: Branch to push. Defaults to the current branch.

set_upstream.description: Set the upstream tracking branch (-u). Default true so first push works.

force_with_lease.description: Force-push using --force-with-lease (safer than --force; refuses if the remote moved unexpectedly). Default false. Never uses a bare --force.

tools.name: git_stash
tools.description: Manage the git stash: save, list, apply, pop, or drop stashed changes. Use when the user wants to set aside uncommitted work, restore stashed work, or inspect the stash list.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

action.description: What to do: save (push) current changes, list stashes, apply/pop a stash (pop also drops it), or drop a stash entry.

message.description: Optional message for the stash entry (used with 'save').

include_untracked.description: Include untracked files when saving (-u). Default false.

ref.description: Stash ref for apply/pop/drop (e.g. 'stash@{1}'). Defaults to the latest stash.

tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Show git working tree status: staged, unstaged, and untracked files. Use when the user asks 'what changed', 'git status', or wants to see the current state of the repo.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

tools.name: pr_checkout
tools.description: Check out a GitHub pull request branch locally using gh CLI. Use when the user wants to test, run, or review a PR's code locally rather than just reading the diff. Requires gh CLI to be authenticated.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

number.description: PR number to check out. Required.

detach.description: Check out in detached HEAD state instead of creating a local branch. Default false.

tools.name: pr_create
tools.description: Create a GitHub pull request using gh CLI. Use when the user asks to open a PR, create a pull request, or submit changes for review. Requires gh CLI to be authenticated. The assistant should confirm title and body with the user before calling.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

body.description: PR description body (markdown). Can be multi-line.

base.description: Base branch to merge into. Defaults to the repo default branch.

head.description: Head branch to merge from. Defaults to current branch.

labels.description: Labels to apply to the PR.

reviewers.description: GitHub usernames to request as reviewers.

tools.name: pr_list
tools.description: List open pull requests in a GitHub repo using gh CLI. Use when the user asks to see PRs, check what is open, or review pending pull requests.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

state.description: PR state to filter by. Default 'open'.

limit.description: Maximum number of PRs to return. Default 20.

author.description: Filter by author (GitHub username or @me for the authenticated user).

tools.name: pr_merge
tools.description: Merge a GitHub pull request using gh CLI. Use when the user asks to merge a PR. WARNING: This is a destructive action that modifies the target branch. The assistant MUST confirm with the user before merging.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

delete_branch.description: Delete the head branch after merging. Default true.

admin.description: Use admin privileges to merge even if CI checks fail or reviews are pending.

tools.name: pr_review
tools.description: Review a GitHub pull request: fetch the diff, CI status, and review comments. Use when the user asks to review a PR, check CI status, or see what a PR changes.

repo_path.description: Path to the git repository. Defaults to the assistant working directory.

include_diff.description: Include the full diff in the output. Default true.
